summary: Excellent sound with very little hiss and a rare early acoustic set that kicks off an otherwise classic ’69 show.
review: Fans rave about the crystal‑clear audio – virtually no hiss – and love the ad‑hoc acoustic opener, complete with a Mason’s tease and first‑time acoustic renditions. The electric portion keeps the momentum going with tight performances of Good Lovin' and Lovelight, making this one of the standout 1969 recordings.

ai_rating.rationale: All reviews praise both the musical performance and the audio quality; the show is highlighted as a rare early acoustic set with strong electric songs, leading to a high combined rating.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Very little hiss, clear mix; reviewers note excellent sound quality throughout the recording.

show_quality.setlist_flow: The show opens with an unplanned acoustic set caused by Phil’s traffic delay, which adds a unique charm before transitioning into a strong electric second half; the flow is praised as both surprising and effective.

band_member_comments.Jerry: His humor shines when he admits not knowing all the words to the Wake Up Little Susie tease; no specific criticism of his playing.
band_member_comments.Phil: Late due to traffic, prompting the acoustic set; no direct comments on his bass work.
